7126	  The \emph{augmented {\v C}ech complex} is the cochain complex
7127	  \[
7128	    0 \to \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{C}^0 \to \mathcal{C}^1 \to \cdots
7129	  \]
7130	  in \(X.\mathrm{Modules}\) obtained by prepending the coefficient sheaf \(\mathcal{F}\) in degree \(0\)

7133	  \(\varepsilon \cdot d^0 = 0\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_augmentation_comp_d}. Concretely its degree-\(0\)
7134	  term is \(\mathcal{F}\) and its degree-\((p+1)\) term is \(\mathcal{C}^p\). This is the object whose
7135	  exactness is the content of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_augmented_resolution}.
7136	\end{definition}
7137	
7138	\begin{lemma}
7139	[Sheafification kills a presheaf that is locally bijective to a locally-zero one]
7140	  \label{lem:sheafify_kills_locally_zero}
7141	  \lean{CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_W,
7142	    CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_W_isZero,
7143	    CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_isLocallyBijective}
7144	  \textit{Project-bespoke site-theory lemma (pure Mathlib site theory).}
7145	  Let \(J\) be a Grothendieck topology on a site \(\mathcal{C}\) and let \(J.W\) denote the
7146	  class of \emph{locally bijective} maps of presheaves --- the local-isomorphism class for
7147	  \(J\), i.e.\ the maps inverted by sheafification. Then:
7148	  \begin{enumerate}
7149	    \item Sheafification \(\operatorname{presheafToSheaf}\) carries every map in \(J.W\) to an
7150	      isomorphism; consequently it sends a presheaf that is locally bijective to a presheaf
7151	      with zero sheafification to a presheaf with zero sheafification. Precisely, if
7152	      \(\varphi : P \to Q\) lies in \(J.W\) and the sheafification of \(Q\) is the zero
7153	      sheaf, then the sheafification of \(P\) is the zero sheaf.
7154	    \item In particular, if \(P\) admits a locally bijective map to the zero presheaf --- for
7155	      instance if \(P\) is locally zero, i.e.\ vanishes on a basis of the topology --- then
7156	      the sheafification of \(P\) is the zero sheaf.
7157	    \item For an abelian-valued presheaf the locally-bijective hypothesis is the conjunction
7158	      of local injectivity and local surjectivity of the comparison map; a map that is both
7159	      locally injective and locally surjective lies in \(J.W\).
7160	  \end{enumerate}
7161	\end{lemma}
7162	\begin{proof}
7163	  Sheafification \(\operatorname{presheafToSheaf} : \widehat{\mathcal{C}} \to
7164	  \operatorname{Sh}(J)\) inverts exactly the maps of the class \(J.W\): by definition,
7165	  \(\varphi \in J.W\) if and only if \(\operatorname{presheafToSheaf}(\varphi)\) is an
7166	  isomorphism. Hence for \(\varphi : P \to Q\) in \(J.W\) the induced map
7167	  \(\operatorname{sheafify}(P) \to \operatorname{sheafify}(Q)\) is an isomorphism, and
7168	  being a zero object transports along an isomorphism: if \(\operatorname{sheafify}(Q)\)
7169	  is zero then so is \(\operatorname{sheafify}(P)\). This proves (1); part (2) is the
7170	  special case \(Q = 0\) (the zero presheaf, whose sheafification is the zero sheaf),
7171	  and a presheaf that vanishes on a basis admits such a locally bijective map to \(0\).
7172	  For (3), sheafification is additive, and a map of abelian presheaves that is
7173	  simultaneously locally injective and locally surjective is a local isomorphism,
7174	  i.e.\ lies in \(J.W\); applying (1) then collapses its sheafification.
7175	\end{proof}

7177	\begin{lemma}
7178	[A faithful zero-preserving functor reflects zero objects]
7179	  \label{lem:isZero_of_faithful_preservesZeroMorphisms}

7181	  Let \(F : \mathcal{C} \to \mathcal{D}\) be a functor between categories with zero
7182	  morphisms that is \emph{faithful} and \emph{preserves zero morphisms}. If the image
7183	  \(F(Y)\) of an object \(Y\) is a zero object of \(\mathcal{D}\), then \(Y\) is a zero
7184	  object of \(\mathcal{C}\).
7185	\end{lemma}
7186	\begin{proof}
7187	  An object \(Y\) is a zero object if and only if its identity morphism equals its zero
7188	  endomorphism, \(\mathrm{id}_Y = 0\). Applying this characterization to \(F(Y)\), the
7189	  hypothesis gives \(\mathrm{id}_{F(Y)} = 0\). Since \(F\) preserves identities and zero
7190	  morphisms, \(F(\mathrm{id}_Y) = \mathrm{id}_{F(Y)} = 0 = F(0)\); faithfulness of \(F\)
7191	  then makes the map on hom-sets injective, so \(\mathrm{id}_Y = 0\), whence \(Y\) is a
7192	  zero object.
7193	\end{proof}
7194

7199	  \uses{lem:sheafify_kills_locally_zero}

7201	  presheaf of abelian groups on \(\mathcal{C}\). Suppose that for every object \(U\) there
7202	  is a covering sieve \(S \in J(U)\) all of whose members \(g : V \to U\) land on an object

7204	  sheaf.
7205	\end{lemma}

7209	  Lemma~\ref{lem:sheafify_kills_locally_zero}. Let \(Z\) be the constant presheaf with
7210	  value the zero group; its sheafification is the zero sheaf. The zero map \(0 : Q \to Z\)
7211	  is locally injective --- on each covering sieve furnished by the hypothesis the source
7212	  sections \(Q(V)\) are subsingletons, so the comparison is injective locally --- and it
7213	  is locally surjective because the target is objectwise a zero object. Hence
7214	  \(0 : Q \to Z\) is locally bijective, i.e.\ lies in the local-isomorphism class
7215	  \(J.W\), and part (3) followed by part (1) of
7216	  Lemma~\ref{lem:sheafify_kills_locally_zero} collapses the sheafification of \(Q\) to the

7288	
7289	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "It suffices to show that (\ref{equation-extended})
7290	% is exact after localizing at a prime $\mathfrak p$, see

7292	% In fact we will show that the extended complex localized
7293	% at $\mathfrak p$ is homotopic to zero.
7294	% There exists an index $i$ such that $f_i \not \in \mathfrak p$.

7297	% Let us define a homotopy ... by the rule

7299	% (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L80--110).
7300	\begin{proof}
7301	  \uses{def:cech_augmented_complex, lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy,
7302	    lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy_spec, lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise,
7303	    lem:cech_free_eval_engine_iso, lem:cech_free_eval_nonempty,
7304	    lem:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology, lem:sheafify_kills_locally_zero,
7305	    lem:isZero_of_faithful_preservesZeroMorphisms,
7306	    lem:isZero_presheafToSheaf_of_locally_isZero}
7307	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
7308	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof).}
7309	  We prove exactness of the augmented {\v C}ech complex of
7310	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_augmented_complex} in \(X.\mathrm{Modules}\) by showing that each homology
7311	  object \(\mathcal{H}^p\) (\(p \geq 1\), together with the augmentation node) is the zero object. The
7312	  argument works entirely with sections and sheafification, never with stalks.
7313	
7314	  \emph{Step 1: reflect through the forgetful functor to abelian sheaves.} The reflection is carried
7315	  out through the forgetful functor \(\operatorname{SheafOfModules.toSheaf} : X.\mathrm{Modules} \to
7316	  \operatorname{Sh}(X, \mathbf{Ab})\) from sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules to sheaves of abelian
7317	  groups. This functor is faithful and additive, hence preserves zero morphisms; a faithful additive
7318	  functor reflects the property of an object being zero. It therefore suffices to prove that the image
7319	  \(\operatorname{SheafOfModules.toSheaf}(\mathcal{H}^p)\) of each homology object is zero in the
7320	  category of abelian sheaves.
7321	
7322	  \emph{Step 2: the homology sheaf is the sheafification of the presheaf homology.} By the

7324	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology}, the degree-\(p\) homology of the complex of

7326	  \emph{presheaves}. The presheaf homology evaluated at an open \(V\) is the homology of the section
7327	  complex \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})(V)\) --- the {\v C}ech cochain complex
7328	  of \(\mathcal{F}\) over the cover restricted to \(V\) --- so the presheaf in question is
7329	  \(V \mapsto \check{H}^p(V, \mathcal{F})\).
7330	
7331	  \emph{Step 3: the presheaf homology vanishes locally, via the prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) homotopy.}
7332	  This presheaf is not zero as a presheaf, but it vanishes on a basis. The relevant basis is the family
7333	  of opens \(V\) contained in some single cover element \(U_i\); since \(\{U_i\}\) covers \(X\), every
7334	  open is covered by such \(V\) (intersect with the \(U_i\)), so this family is cofinal among all opens.
7335	  Fix such a \(V\) with \(V \subseteq U_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}}\). The section complex

7382	      packaging this is the homology of the complex obtained by applying the evaluation-at-\(V\) functor

7416	  \mathcal{C}^0\) is handled by the \emph{same} prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) homotopy of Step 3. Over an
7417	  open \(V \subseteq U_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}}\), the contracting homotopy extends to the augmentation term:
7418	  the degree-\((-1)\)/degree-\(0\) component of \(h\) sends a section \(s \in \prod_s \mathcal{F}(U_s
7419	  \cap V)\) to its \(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\)-component \(s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} \in \mathcal{F}(U_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}}
7420	  \cap V) = \mathcal{F}(V)\), so the identity \(d \circ h + h \circ d = \mathrm{id}\) holds at the

7427	  Step 3 makes the augmentation-node homology zero. Together with Steps 1--3, every homology object of
7428	  the augmented complex is zero, so the complex is exact and the {\v C}ech nerve of
7429	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve} is a resolution of \(\mathcal{F}\) in \(X.\mathrm{Modules}\). In

7435	  exact if and only if it is exact after localizing at each prime \(\mathfrak{p}\), and for each
